description: 'kelua' is the earlier part of the day after dawn and before midday. It is one of Phi's natural stations, broad enough to follow local light, season, and ordinary practice rather than a fixed clock interval.
articulatory_notes: Press the back of the tongue against the soft palate for 'k' and release into middle-height front 'e'. Touch the tongue tip to the alveolar ridge for 'l' and draw the tongue back into stressed high-back rounded 'u'. Release the lip rounding and open the jaw for the separate final central 'a'.

semantic_domains.temporal: the early daily span following dawn and leading to midday
